thread.title: City of Kennewick celebrates groundbreaking at Vista Field
thread.title_full: City of Kennewick celebrates groundbreaking at Vista Field

author: Aiden Whitaker
title: City of Kennewick celebrates groundbreaking at Vista Field
text: KENNEWICK, Wash. — Leaders with the City of Kennewick, Benton County, and the Port of Kennewick joined together to host a groundbreaking event at Vista Field on Friday, November 22nd. Kuki Izakaya Japanese Restaurant is the first business to begin construction at Vista Field, part of the Port of Kennewick's masterplan to turn the area into a community hub that will feature other businesses, housing, and entertainment in a condensed walkable area. They're super excited, and the fact that it's a small business here in our community ... not just them being a local small business, but the contractor is local, their real estate agent is local, their architect is local," said Amber Hanchette, director of real estate for the port. "So, this is, again, very much a community project." Vista Field was a small airport in central Kennewick before local leaders decided to close it to pave the way for what would become the Vista Field Master Plan, which is finally becoming a reality. Remnants of the airfield are still visible, including part of the runway.
